notes:
Pack structure is the Diamond & Pearl / Platinum-era standard confirmed by Bulbapedia's Booster_pack article: 10 cards/pack = 4 common + 3 uncommon + 1 reverse-holo + 1 rare-or-better. Box = 36 packs, NO ETB (vintage 2009). NOTE this contradicts the task's 9-card DP template — the sourced era structure for the Platinum block is 10 cards, so packSize=10.

Set buckets given: rare-holo 17, rare 26, uncommon 42, common 39, lv-x 6, secret-rare 3. The set has exactly 6 Level X (122-127) and 3 Secret Rares / "Shiny" star-H cards (128-130 Electabuzz/Hitmonchan/Scyther), matching the buckets (Serebii/Bulbapedia).

RARE SLOT (chase) distribution, mapped onto the available buckets:
- Level X: ~1:18 packs in the Platinum block (Flipside Gaming) = ~2 per box; community (Flipside, search-summarized PokeBeach card-ratios) converge on ~2 Level X/box for Platinum-era. 2/36 = 0.0556.
- Secret rare (Shiny): reported Actual Rarity ~1:36 packs (Flipside Gaming) = ~1 per box. 1/36 = 0.0278.
- Remaining 0.9166 of the rare slot split rare-holo vs rare. No published per-set holo:non-holo box ratio exists, so I inferred it from the set's printable holo:non-holo pool (17 rare-holo : 26 rare = 43 total): rare-holo 0.9166*17/43 = 0.362, rare 0.9166*26/43 = 0.5546. This split is an estimate, not a sourced figure — the main driver of the 'low' confidence.

REVERSE-HOLO SLOT: the reverse can be a Common, Uncommon, or Rare (Bulbapedia). Modeled across buckets proportional to the printable C/U/R pool (common 39, uncommon 42, rare 26, rare-holo 17; pool 124): common 0.315, uncommon 0.339, rare 0.210, rare-holo 0.136. (Secret rares and Level X are not part of the reverse pool.)

UNCOMMON SLOT x3 and COMMON SLOT x4 are 100% their bucket.

Confidence = low: no large per-set published pull-rate study for pl1; era-average rates (1:18 Level X, 1:36 Shiny) applied with community corroboration, and the holo/non-holo split is inferred from bucket counts. Box price reflects a sealed-vintage collector premium, so EV/booster will run well below box price — expected, rates not inflated.
